mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase borrowing costs and discount rates, directly reducing present value of equity cash flows and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean during rate hikes with p-value less than 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.0 percent, it signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending capacity, causing S&P 500 forward returns to turn negative over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 65 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in real personal spending and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and investment demand expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive changes in RSAFS and HOUST yield negative forward returns in greater than 55 percent of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during post-recession recovery phases reflects accelerating factory output and corporate earnings momentum, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases during recovery phases fail to predict positive 5-day returns with a Rank IC below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation above 0.4 percent monthly triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL spikes above 0.4 percent monthly are followed by positive 20-day returns in greater than 60 percent of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total nonfarm payrolls and falling unemployment rates confirms robust labor market absorption, supporting corporate revenue growth and yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence signal produces negative forward returns in greater than 50 percent of triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real GDP growth directly correlates with expanding corporate profit margins and aggregate demand, consistently generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show zero or negative correlation with 20-day forward returns across all rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in industrial production signal imminent supply-side contraction and earnings downgrades, forcing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Consecutive INDPRO declines are followed by positive 5-day returns in greater than 55 percent of cases.
